nodes.title: Hearing Rehabilitation
nodes.description: Examination of the impact of hearing loss on development and its overall effects on communication. Survey of management considerations, sensory devices, speech reading, and auditory training. Credit not awarded for both CSD 3720 and CSD 5720. Prerequisite: CSD 3710.
nodes.other_requirements: CSD undergraduate students only

nodes.title: Cultural Diversity & Develop
nodes.description: Introductory courses or seminars on topics beyond the scope of existing departmental offerings. See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior, Senior standing or with instructor permission

nodes.title: Clinic Practicum Supplemental
nodes.description: Supervised practicum experiences with children and adults presenting disorders of speech, hearing, and language. Prerequisites: CSD 6200, CSD 6210; Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student. Winter Session for Continuing CSD Grad Student Clinic; Course dates 12/26/25 - 01/9/26; Internship experience off-campus; Instructor permission required. Open to degree and non-degree students

nodes.title: Assmt & Treatmt of Stuttering
nodes.description: Study of adult and child fluency disorders with focus on symptomatology, etiology, diagnosis, and rehabilitation of people with stuttering disorders including cluttering and neurogenetic populations. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.

nodes.title: Intrdsc Sem Neurodev Disabil 2
nodes.description: Seminar exploring interdisciplinary process, collaborative teaming, teaching, cultural competence and family-centered care as they relate to children and families affected by neurodevelopmental and related disabilities, including ASD. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Dev of Spoken Language
nodes.description: Speech and language acquisition interpreted in light of current learning and cognitive theory, linguistic theory, and methods of linguistic analysis.

nodes.title: Adv Topics in Clin Aud & SLP
nodes.description: Provides advanced exploration of the professions and clinical work of audiologists and speech language pathologists. Skills and knowledge related to ethical issues, person/family centered care, and cultural competence are practiced. Guided observations review specific clinical skills along with their application in practice. Prerequisite: CSD 1210; Sophomore standing. Pre/Co-requisite: CSD 1200.
nodes.other_requirements: Sophomore standing; CSD majors only; Minimum sophomore standing; Course meets in person on Tuesdays (2 credits); Weekly clinical observations (1 credit); Fee for HIPAA/OSHA training: $7.00

nodes.title: Language Disorders
nodes.description: Identification, evaluation, and rehabilitation procedures for children with language disabilities. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student. CSD graduate students only

nodes.title: Introduction to Audiology
nodes.description: Survey of hearing and the nature and causes of hearing impairment. Includes an orientation to assessment procedures and rationales, hearing screening and counseling considerations. Prerequisites: CSD 2010, CSD 2210.
nodes.other_requirements: CSD majors only; Juniors, seniors only

nodes.title: The Human Body
nodes.description: For nonscience majors. Introduction to basic human anatomy and organ system physiology emphasizing normal homeostatic mechanisms and the changes that accompany common disorders and diseases. No laboratory. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1155 and BIOL 1150.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Cognition & Language
nodes.description: Study of cognition and language in terms of mental representation models; contemporary models of memory, as well as capacity theories of language comprehension and production. Credit not awarded for both CSD 3480 and CSD 5480. Prerequisite: CSD 2010.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both CSD 3480 and CSD 5480.

nodes.title: Intro to Disordered Comm
nodes.description: Survey of language, speech, and hearing disorders, emphasizing the importance of understanding such disorders as a part of the fuller understanding of human behavior.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Working with Lang Disorders
nodes.description: Evidence-based practice and response to intervention strategies, screening and intervention for language differences; diverse populations. Complete 50 hours practicum. Prerequisite: CSD 3250.
nodes.other_requirements: Complete 50 hours practicum. Open to degree and non-degree students SLP, SLPA, Post-Bac, PACE program participants only; Approved CSD majors with permission; Online Asynchronous This course is open to students in the post-graduate SLPA certificate program and those who are in the undergraduate SLPA certificate program after taking CSD 3250. It also requires completion of the clinical practicum (100 hours total, 80 of them involving direct clinical contact).

nodes.title: Comm Diff & Dis in Media
nodes.description: Analysis of the portrayal of individuals with communication differences and disorders in the media and how this influences our perceptions and opinions. Guest speakers, shared experiences, classroom discussions, and the viewing of popular films.

nodes.title: Culture of Disability
nodes.description: Examines the social and cultural experience of disability in different times and cultures. As an introduction to Disability Studies, topics covered will include foundational concepts/vocabulary, the influence of cultural beliefs, personal narratives, education, healthcare, social services, self-advocacy and the disability rights movement. Credit not awarded for both CSD 3200 and CSD 5740. Pre/Co-requisites: EDSP 1050, ASL 1990; or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: EDSP 3250.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Clinic Practicum Study 1
nodes.description: First in the clinical practicum sequence in the Department of Communication Sciences and Disorders (CSD). Graduate students will directly apply new knowledge of clinical preparation and management to provide services to individuals with communication disorders in the University of Vermont Eleanor M. Luse Center for Communication. Pre/Co-requisites: CSD 6200; Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student. CSD First Year Graduate Students Only

nodes.title: Intrdsc Sem Neurodev Disabil I
nodes.description: Seminar with focus on cultural competence and family-centered care, interdisciplinary collaborative teaming, policy and health disparities related to children and families affected by neurodevelopmental and related disabilities, including ASD. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Graduate students only; VT Lend Trainees and Fellows register for CSD 6110 OL2; MPH graduate students should register for CSD 6110 OL3; Instructor permission required; Asynchronous Online Course PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Accelerated Biology
nodes.description: Selected topics from the full year of introductory biology, compressed into one semester. For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. Pre/co-requisite: CHEM 1400, CHEM 1405, or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. By invitation only; for FTFY students with AP score of 4. Instructor permission required. Must register for a BCOR 1425 lab. Must be enrolled in CHEM 1400 if have no CHEM credit.

nodes.title: Swallowing Disorders
nodes.description: Introduction to normal and disordered swallowing function across the life span including etiologies, signs/symptoms of dysphagia, diagnostic procedures and treatment within an interdisciplinary model. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student. CSD 1st year graduate students only; Open to Degree and PACE students. Students are required to complete an online training program, MBSImP, which is an additional cost to students of $79.

nodes.title: Clinic Preparation&Management
nodes.description: Principles of behavioral observation, analysis and modification as they apply to the assessment and remediation of communication disorders.

nodes.title: Linguistics for Clinicians
nodes.description: Linguistic concepts, applications to clinical contexts. Topics include language components, language processing in the brain, individual differences and disorders, dialects, normal and disordered language acquisition.
nodes.other_requirements: SLPA, Post-Bac SLPA, PACE students only until restrictions are removed, or instructor permission; Asynchronous Online Course

nodes.title: Voice Disorders
nodes.description: Study of normal and abnormal laryngeal anatomy and physiology as they relate to diagnoses and treatment of a wide variety of vocal pathologies. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.

nodes.title: Intro Cognitive Neuroscience
nodes.description: This course introduces students to the organization, structures and functions of the human central nervous system. Higher cognitive and linguistic behaviors are emphasized. Prerequisite: Human Biology course such as one of the following: BIOL 1105, BIOL 1155, BCOR 1400, BCOR 1450, or ANPS 1190.
nodes.other_requirements: CSD Junior and senior students only; Open to NSCI students, as well as others with permission; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Neurogenic Comm. Disorders 1
nodes.description: The study of linguistic and cognitive-communication impairments associated with stroke and other acquired neurogenic communication disorders. Aphasia, Right Hemisphere Communication Disorders (RHD) and communication disorders associated with dementia are explored. Emphasis on the principles and procedures of assessment and intervention are emphasized. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student. CSD 1st year Grad students only

nodes.title: Research Methods II
nodes.description: Students will critically review the professional literature in preparation for carrying out a systemic review.
nodes.other_requirements: CSD 2nd year Graduate students only. PACE students by permission and override. Pre and post-instruction assignments required.

nodes.title: Introduction to Phonetics
nodes.description: Linguistic, acoustic, and articulatory phonetics applied to the description of speech. Stresses use of the International Phonetic Alphabet with English, foreign languages, and disordered speech.
nodes.other_requirements: SLP, SLPA, Post-Bac, PACE program participants only or instructor permission

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Cultural Effects on Developmt
nodes.description: Focuses on how different kinds of diversity (including race, ability, gender identity, etc.) influence development in children cognitively, linguistically, and socially. Drawing broadly from disciplines like developmental psychology, education, and neuroscience, covers topics including cultural differences in parenting, bilingual and bidialectical development, aspects of literacy, etc.

nodes.title: Speech & Hearing Science
nodes.description: Structure and function of the respiratory, phonatory, articulatory, and hearing systems, coupled with models of speech and hearing as part of human communication. Prerequisites: Communication Sciences & Disorders, Education major or minor, Neuroscience major; minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements:
minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission. CSD, ECP, ECSP, ELK6, FCSE, PE, SEE, NEUR majors and CSD minors only;
Must register for one of CSD 2010 labs L01-L03;

nodes.title: Working with Speech Disorders
nodes.description: Speech language pathology assistants' roles in schools working with speech disorders; health/safety, special education and HIPAA issues; observation, data collection, and collaboration skills. Complete 50 hour practicum. Prerequisites: CSD 1200, CSD 1220, CSD 1940; CSD 1230 or LING 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Must be in SLPA cohort; Instructor permission for seniors who have completed CSD 1200; Undergraduates can also register for this course with instructor permission on August 15th. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Spch Snd Disorders in Children
nodes.description: Etiology, diagnosis, pathology, and habilitation and rehabilitation of articulation of speech. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student. First year CSD Graduate degree students only; Degrees other than CSD with instructor permission; Open to Degree and PACE students;

nodes.title: Clinic Practicum Study 4
nodes.description: Fourth in the clinical practicum sequence in the Department of Communication Sciences and Disorders (CSD). Students will complete an intensive off-campus practicum assignment. In addition, graduate students will be assigned direct time in the clinic working with clients, a weekly meeting time with assigned clinical supervisor and work outside of those times to plan for sessions, review videos for data collection, self-reflection, and necessary clinical documentation. Prerequisites: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student; B or better in CSD 6210, CSD 6220, or CSD 6230.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student; B or better in prerequisite courses. CSD Grad Students in 2nd year clinic only; This class meets 5:30-6:45 pm: Aug 29, Sept 5, Sept 12, Sept 19, Sept 26; Post-instruction material required until Oct 15th; Course Fees: $81.00

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for the Master's Thesis.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Neurogenic Comm. Disorders 2
nodes.description: Covers the study of motor speech disorders associated with damage to the central or peripheral nervous system. Cognitive-communication impairments associated with Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) and related disorders are also explored. Principles and procedures of assessment and intervention are emphasized. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Measurement of Comm Processes
nodes.description: The principles, methods, and problems of psychometrics as applied to the screening and diagnosis of communication processes. Students will describe, critique, and create assessments for reliable and valid measurements of communicative skills. Pre/Co-requisites: CSD 2210 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to CSD and NEUR undergraduate students

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Culture of Disability
nodes.description: Focus on theoretical questions of how societies understand disability and its consequences for social justice, by examining the multiple determinants of the societal construction of disability. Credit not awarded for both CSD 5740 and CSD 3200. Pre/Co-requisites: EDSP 2170, CSD 2010, or ASL 2990; Graduate student; or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: EDSP 5250.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student; or Instructor permission. Juniors, seniors, and Graduate students only;

nodes.title: Clinic Practicum Study 5
nodes.description: Supervised practicum experiences with children and adults presenting disorders of speech, hearing, and language. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student. Second year CSD Grad students only.

nodes.title: Iss Aff Persons W/Disabil
nodes.description: Students study the effects of discrimination, advocacy, litigation and sociological perspectives on disabilities. History, current legislation, and family issues for children and adults are emphasized.
nodes.other_requirements: Only open to CESS first-time first-years or by instructor permission; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Behavior Intervention
nodes.description: Discussion of theories and models developed for behavior management, and the translation of those theories into practical intervention techniques, both for the individual student and classroom groups. Emphasis on the use of interpersonal relationships as a means of changing child and youth behavior. Emphasizes culturally responsive practice in relationship to behavioral intervention. Prerequisite: EDSP 1050.
nodes.other_requirements: EDSP majors only; Includes asynchronous online work. All Monday class sessions are asynchronous online. All Wednesdays are in person. This is a hybrid course that will be structured by weekly modules with online work due on Mondays and then weekly face to face class sessions on Wednesdays. This course is open to EDSP majors, minors or instructor permission and is recommend for teacher education special education minors.

nodes.title: Augmentative Communication
nodes.description: An introduction to development and selection of augmentative/alternative communication strategies and systems for persons with severe communication challenges. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student or Instructor permission. CSD on-campus 2nd yr graduate students, Other students with instructor permission; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Clinic Practicum 2 Spring 2026
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: School Based Issues for SLPs
nodes.description: An overview of topics necessary for employment as a school based SLP. Specific topics will be covered related to federal and state special education regulations. Prerequisite: CSD 6200. Co-requisite: CSD 6220.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisite: CSD 6220. First year CSD Graduate students only

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 1
nodes.description: Part I of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereg limited to: BMCS, BIOM, CSD, EMS, EXSC, HSCI, MLS, MRS, NFS, NMT, PE, PHS, PRNU majors only. Degree students only even after major restrictions lifted.

nodes.title: Intro Topics in Clin Aud & SLP
nodes.description: Introduces students to the professions of audiology and speech language pathology. Covers health care related topics relevant to professional practice when working with individuals with communication disorders. Guided observations will introduce specific clinical skills along with their application in practice. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences and Disorders major.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences and Disorders major. CSD majors only; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Human Biology
nodes.description: For nonscience majors. Selected biological topics relevant to humans, such as cancer, human genetics, environmental toxicants; biological concepts necessary for understanding these problems. No laboratory. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1105 and BIOL 1100.
nodes.other_requirements: For nonscience majors. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1105 and BIOL 1100. Course will not count toward BIOL, BISC, NEUR, PBIO or ZOOL majors, and those students are restricted from enrolling. Asynchronous online; Degree students only; Colocated with BIOL 1105 OLR; total combined enrollment = 100

nodes.title: Clinical Phonetics
nodes.description: Transcription of speech using the International Phonetic Alphabet. Speech sound disorders, development, universals, dialects, coarticulation, connected speech, prosody and second-language learning. Prerequisite: Completion of CSD 2010 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Undergraduate CSD students only, all others by instructor permission; Must register for a CSD 2220 lab L01-L02. Three credits in Communication Sciences and Disorders or Linguistics.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 1
nodes.description: Exploring biology from cells to organisms. Topics include origins of life, ancestral organisms, uni- and multi- cellular energetics, evolution of respiration and metabolism, and the genetic code. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1400 and BIOL 1400 or BCOR 1425.

nodes.title: Seminar Lang/Lrng Disabilities
nodes.description: LLD assessment and intervention; oral language-literacy connections. Reading and written language disorders; related challenges. Role of the SLP; evidence-based approaches. Prerequisite: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Communication Sciences & Disorders Graduate student or Instructor permission. Graduate degree students or with instructor permission only. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 2
nodes.description: An evolutionary perspective to exploring biology. Topics include: patterns of inheritance, Darwinian evolution, evolution of biodiversity, ecology of organisms, human effects on biological systems. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1450 and BIOL 1450.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded.

nodes.title: Non-thesis Case Presentation
nodes.description: Students complete a systematic review or research project under the direction of faculty.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required
